source.source_rationale: We identified three types of endothelial cells (E1–E3, Fig. 1), all showing selective expression for GNG11 and SEPW1.

However, comparative analysis between E1 and E2 cells showed that E2 cells express classic endothelial markers such as ACKR1, SELE, TM4SF1, VCAM1, TMEM173, PLVAP, ICAM1, PECAM1, VWF, ADAMTS9 and TFPI.

However, comparative analysis between E1 and E2 cells showed that E2 cells express classic endothelial markers such as ACKR1, SELE, TM4SF1, VCAM1, TMEM173, PLVAP, ICAM1, PECAM1, VWF, ADAMTS9 and TFPI.

To characterize E3, we performed differential gene expression analysis between E3 and the combined population of E1 and E2 cells (Supplementary Table 6) and found LYVE1 among the top expressed genes. LYVE1 is a marker of lymphatic endothelial cells [...]
